project.name: nudity-detector
project.description: A Python-based application designed to detect nudity in images and videos, providing automated explicit content identification for content moderation, safety filters, and compliance checks.

nodes.label: run_gui.py
nodes.description: Entrypoint launcher script for the Nudity Detector GUI application. Delegates execution to src.gui.app.main().

nodes.label: run_helloz_nsfw.py
nodes.description: Entrypoint launcher script for the Helloz NSFW CLI detector. Delegates execution to src.detectors.helloz_nsfw.main().

nodes.label: run_nudenet.py
nodes.description: Entrypoint launcher script for the NudeNet CLI detector. Delegates execution to src.detectors.nudenet.main().

nodes.label: docker-compose.yml
nodes.description: Docker Compose configuration that runs the helloz/nsfw service container on port 6086 with 10 workers. Provides the backend API that the Helloz NSFW detector communicates with.

nodes.label: requirements.txt
nodes.description: Python dependency manifest listing nudenet, vnudenet, openpyxl, Pillow, opencv-python, requests, Send2Trash, and darkdetect. GTK4/libadwaita are noted as system packages.

nodes.label: src/core/__init__.py
nodes.description: Empty __init__.py that marks src/core as a Python package. No symbols are exported at package level.

nodes.label: constants.py
nodes.description: Centralized constants module providing a single source of truth for all configuration values including media extensions, detection classes, thresholds, report settings, GUI layout, and threading parameters.

nodes.label: ScanConfig
nodes.description: Dataclass holding configuration for a scan session: source folder, model name, threshold, and theme. Supports serialization to/from dict.

nodes.label: ReportEntry
nodes.description: Dataclass representing a single detection result entry with file path, media type, model name, threshold, confidence, nudity flag, classes, thumbnail, and date. Supports Excel row conversion and dict serialization.

nodes.label: SessionState
nodes.description: Dataclass representing a complete scan session including version, timestamp, scan config, and list of report entries. Handles nested deserialization in __post_init__.

nodes.label: DetectionResult
nodes.description: Dataclass for raw detection output from a model, including file path, media type, model name, raw data, max confidence, nudity flag, and detected classes. Converts to ReportEntry via to_report_entry().

nodes.label: normalize_threshold()
nodes.description: Normalizes a threshold value from percentage (>1) or decimal (0-1) into the [0,1] range. Returns 0.6 for None input.

nodes.label: threshold_to_percent()
nodes.description: Converts a normalized threshold value to a rounded percentage float.

nodes.label: make_scan_config()
nodes.description: Factory function that creates and returns a ScanConfig dictionary from the given parameters, normalizing the threshold to percent.

nodes.label: create_session_state()
nodes.description: Creates a SessionState dictionary from optional scan config and results, handling dict-to-model conversion.

nodes.label: nudity_report (global)
nodes.description: Global list acting as in-memory cache for detection result dicts, protected by report_lock for thread-safe access.

nodes.label: reset_nudity_report()
nodes.description: Thread-safely clears the global nudity_report list.

nodes.label: get_detected_results()
nodes.description: Filters report data to return only entries where nudity_detected is True, using either provided data or the global nudity_report.

nodes.label: save_nudity_report()
nodes.description: Saves detection results to Excel via ReportManager and persists the session JSON file alongside it.

nodes.label: classify_files_in_folder()
nodes.description: Walks a folder tree and dispatches supported files to image/video classification callables using a configurable thread-pool worker queue, with proper sentinel-based shutdown and timeout handling.

nodes.label: handle_results()
nodes.description: Handles a single detection result: generates thumbnail for detected files, builds a result dict, appends it to the global report under lock, and periodically saves to disk every 500 entries.

nodes.label: detect_with_timeout()
nodes.description: Wraps a detector's detect() call in a daemon thread with a configurable timeout, raising TimeoutError if detection stalls.

nodes.label: open_file()
nodes.description: Opens a file using the OS default application (cross-platform: win32, darwin, linux xdg-open), returning a success/error tuple.

nodes.label: delete_file_safely()
nodes.description: Deletes a file by moving it to trash via send2trash, falling back to permanent deletion if send2trash is unavailable.

nodes.label: process_file()
nodes.description: Routes a single file to classify_image or classify_video based on detected media type, skipping unsupported files.

nodes.label: count_supported_files()
nodes.description: Walks a folder tree and returns the count of all supported media files, used to determine progress bar totals before scanning.

nodes.label: src/detectors/__init__.py
nodes.description: Empty __init__.py that marks src/detectors as a Python package. No symbols exported at package level.

nodes.label: prompt_threshold_percent()
nodes.description: Interactively prompts the user for a threshold percentage and validates/clamps the input against configured min/max bounds.

nodes.label: extract_frames()
nodes.description: Legacy wrapper for backward compatibility that delegates video frame extraction to FrameExtractor with the CLI Helloz NSFW temp prefix.

nodes.label: main() [helloz_nsfw]
nodes.description: CLI entrypoint for the Helloz NSFW detector. Prompts for folder and threshold, then classifies all images (via HTTP POST to the helloz API) and videos (via frame extraction + HTTP POST) in the folder using worker threads, then saves the report.

nodes.label: classify_image() [helloz_nsfw]
nodes.description: Inner function within helloz_nsfw.main that POSTs an image file to the Helloz NSFW API, extracts the nsfw confidence score, and delegates to handle_results().

nodes.label: classify_video() [helloz_nsfw]
nodes.description: Inner function within helloz_nsfw.main that extracts video frames and POSTs each frame to the Helloz NSFW API, tracking max confidence and short-circuiting on threshold breach.

nodes.label: prompt_threshold_percent()
nodes.description: Interactively prompts the user for a threshold percentage and validates/clamps the input against configured min/max bounds (same pattern as helloz_nsfw variant).

nodes.label: simplify_nudenet_results()
nodes.description: Converts raw NudeDetector detection records (list of dicts with 'label' and 'score') into a simplified list of {class, score} dicts.

nodes.label: get_nudenet_confidence()
nodes.description: Extracts the maximum confidence score from a NudeDetector result, considering only classes in NUDITY_CLASSES_STRICT.

nodes.label: main() [nudenet]
nodes.description: CLI entrypoint for the NudeNet detector. Prompts for folder and threshold, initializes NudeDetector, then classifies images and videos (with frame extraction) using worker threads, and saves the final report.

nodes.label: classify_image() [nudenet]
nodes.description: Inner function within nudenet.main that runs NudeDetector.detect() on an image, computes confidence, and delegates to handle_results().

nodes.label: classify_video() [nudenet]
nodes.description: Inner function within nudenet.main that extracts video frames, runs NudeDetector.detect() on each, accumulates max confidence, and short-circuits on threshold breach before calling handle_results().

nodes.label: gui/__init__
nodes.description: Empty package init for the gui subpackage.

nodes.label: NudityDetectorWindow
nodes.description: Main GTK4/libadwaita application window that composes all mixin classes. Manages scan state, UI construction, config persistence, theme, activity log, and scan control.

nodes.label: NudityDetectorApp
nodes.description: Adw.Application subclass that bootstraps the app, creates the window, and handles close-request with in-progress scan guard.

nodes.label: main()
nodes.description: Top-level entry-point that instantiates NudityDetectorApp and calls app.run().

nodes.label: DialogsMixin
nodes.description: Mixin providing reusable alert-dialog helpers (_show_error, _show_warning, _ask_yes_no) via Adw.AlertDialog.

nodes.label: PreviewMixin
nodes.description: Mixin providing thumbnail loading and preview-panel update logic, sourcing images from disk or base64 fallback, and converting PIL images to GdkPixbuf.

nodes.label: ResultItem
nodes.description: GObject-backed data model for a single row in the ColumnView results table, holding file name, media type, confidence, model name, and path.

nodes.label: ResultsMixin
nodes.description: Mixin handling results-table population (full rebuild and incremental append), row selection, and row actions (open file, open location, delete).

nodes.label: ScanRunItem
nodes.description: GObject model for a single row in the scan-history ColumnView, holding dir name, date, model, result count, source folder, session and report paths.

nodes.label: ScanHistoryMixin
nodes.description: Mixin that builds the All Scans tab, reads the reports/ directory to populate a scan-history ColumnView, and provides Load, Export, Delete and Clear-All actions.

nodes.label: ScanningMixin
nodes.description: Mixin encapsulating the full scan lifecycle: start/stop, NudeNet and Helloz NSFW classifier factories, video frame extraction, multi-threaded file processing, progress pulse, and intermediate result flushing.

nodes.label: SessionMixin
nodes.description: Mixin managing session persistence: save/load dialogs (xlsx and JSON), auto-load on startup, report opening, and scan-config/session-state builders.

nodes.label: src/__init__
nodes.description: Empty top-level package init for the src package.

nodes.label: processing/__init__
nodes.description: Empty package init for the processing subpackage.

nodes.label: detect_media_type()
nodes.description: Detects media type ('image', 'video', or 'unknown') from a file's extension using constants.IMAGE_EXTENSIONS and constants.VIDEO_EXTENSIONS.

nodes.label: is_supported_file()
nodes.description: Returns True if the file has a supported image or video extension.

nodes.label: FrameExtractor
nodes.description: Extracts video frames at a configurable sampling rate using OpenCV, storing them in a temporary directory (with optional /dev/shm placement). Provides cleanup() for temp-dir removal.

nodes.label: ThumbnailGenerator
nodes.description: Static-method class that generates base64-encoded PNG thumbnails from image files (via PIL) or video files (via OpenCV + PIL), with auto media-type detection.

nodes.label: reporting/__init__
nodes.description: Empty package init for the reporting subpackage.

nodes.label: ReportManager
nodes.description: Static-method class that handles all report file I/O: reading/writing Excel (.xlsx) reports with embedded thumbnails, saving/loading session JSON files, and validating report directories.

layers.name: Entrypoint
layers.description: Top-level launcher scripts and application entry functions. These modules bootstrap execution by delegating to the appropriate subsystem (GUI app or CLI detector). No business logic lives here.

layers.name: UI (GTK4 GUI)
layers.description: GTK4/libadwaita application layer. Composed of an Adw.Application subclass, a main window that assembles mixin classes, and mixin modules covering dialogs, preview thumbnails, results table, scan history, scanning lifecycle, and session persistence. Responsible for all user-facing interaction and event handling.

layers.name: Detector Backends
layers.description: Dual detection backends: NudeNet (local ML model) and Helloz NSFW (remote HTTP API). Each backend provides CLI entrypoints, image/video classification functions, confidence extraction helpers, and threshold prompting. Both backends use the shared processing and core layers.

layers.name: Processing Pipeline
layers.description: Media processing infrastructure: media type detection, file eligibility checking, video frame extraction via OpenCV, thumbnail generation via PIL/OpenCV, thread-pool orchestration for batch scanning, per-file routing, detection timeout wrapping, and progress counting.

layers.name: Reporting
layers.description: Report persistence layer. ReportManager handles reading and writing Excel (.xlsx) files with embedded thumbnails and JSON session files. save_nudity_report orchestrates serializing in-memory detection results to disk.

layers.name: Core Domain & Utilities
layers.description: Foundation layer: domain data models (ScanConfig, ReportEntry, SessionState, DetectionResult), application-wide constants, threshold normalization utilities, global in-memory report state with thread-safe access, session/config factories, and cross-platform file operations (open, safe delete).

layers.name: Infrastructure & Configuration
layers.description: Project-level configuration and deployment infrastructure. Includes the Docker Compose file that runs the Helloz NSFW API service container, and the Python requirements manifest specifying all package dependencies.

crossCuttingConcerns.name: Thread Safety
crossCuttingConcerns.description: Multiple components coordinate concurrent access to shared state. The global nudity_report list is protected by a lock, detect_with_timeout wraps detector calls in daemon threads, classify_files_in_folder manages a thread-pool with sentinel-based shutdown, and ScanningMixin/ScanHistoryMixin both perform background threading within the GUI.

crossCuttingConcerns.name: Constants & Configuration
crossCuttingConcerns.description: The constants module acts as a single source of truth consumed across all layers — detectors, processing, UI, reporting, and core models all reference it for thresholds, extensions, model names, GUI settings, and result field names.

crossCuttingConcerns.name: Session Persistence
crossCuttingConcerns.description: Session and report data flows through multiple layers. SessionState/ReportEntry (core) are serialized by ReportManager (reporting), orchestrated via save_nudity_report (processing/utils), and surfaced in the GUI through SessionMixin and ScanHistoryMixin.

crossCuttingConcerns.name: Thumbnail Generation
crossCuttingConcerns.description: Thumbnails are generated at detection time (ThumbnailGenerator, handle_results), embedded into Excel reports (ReportManager), stored as base64 in session state, and rendered back in the GUI preview panel (PreviewMixin).

tour.title: Nudity Detector Codebase Tour
tour.description: This project scans image and video folders for explicit content using either a local NudeNet model or a Helloz NSFW HTTP service, then saves results as Excel reports plus session JSON. Most of the application logic is shared across a GTK desktop GUI and two small CLI entrypoints, with the main flow moving from scan setup to file classification, result aggregation, thumbnail generation, and report persistence.

stops.title: Start at the launchers
stops.description: The repository exposes three top-level ways in: run_gui.py for the desktop app, run_nudenet.py for local-model CLI scanning, and run_helloz_nsfw.py for API-backed CLI scanning. These files are intentionally tiny, so they are the fastest way to see the supported execution modes before diving into the real implementation modules.
stops.nextStopReason: From the launcher, the most important path for day-to-day development is the GUI application entrypoint it delegates to.

stops.title: Follow the GUI entrypoint into the application shell
stops.description: src/gui/app.py:main() instantiates NudityDetectorApp and starts the GTK/libadwaita event loop. This is the handoff point where the lightweight launcher becomes a long-lived desktop application with window lifecycle, activation, and shutdown behavior.
stops.nextStopReason: Once the app is running, the key object to understand is the main window that composes almost all user-facing behavior.

stops.title: Understand the main composition root
stops.description: NudityDetectorWindow is the central GUI object and the closest thing this project has to an application coordinator. It combines scan control, results display, preview behavior, session persistence, dialogs, configuration, and history management by inheriting several mixins, so it is the best place to understand how the UI is assembled into one workflow.
stops.nextStopReason: The window delegates most interesting scanning behavior to a dedicated mixin, which is where the core control flow begins.

stops.title: Study the scan lifecycle orchestration
stops.description: ScanningMixin owns the end-to-end scanning lifecycle: starting and stopping scans, selecting NudeNet vs Helloz NSFW, wiring classifier functions, tracking progress, and flushing intermediate results back into the UI. If you want to change how scans are scheduled, how progress updates work, or how the GUI coordinates background work, this is the first module to inspect.
stops.nextStopReason: Its main dependency is the shared folder-processing utility that actually fans file work out across worker threads.

stops.title: See how batch work is distributed
stops.description: classify_files_in_folder() in src/core/utils.py is the shared orchestration layer used by both CLI detectors and the GUI scanning flow. It walks the target folder tree, routes each supported file through process_file(), manages the worker queue and sentinel shutdown, and provides the concurrency backbone for large scans.
stops.nextStopReason: That batching logic depends on a small media-processing layer to decide what each file is and how video content should be sampled.

stops.title: Learn the media-processing primitives
stops.description: src/processing/media_processor.py contains the low-level helpers that make mixed media scanning possible. detect_media_type() classifies files by extension, FrameExtractor samples video frames into a temp directory for detector reuse, and ThumbnailGenerator produces preview/report thumbnails, so this layer bridges raw files and higher-level detector/report code.
stops.nextStopReason: With media routing understood, the next step is to compare the two detector implementations that plug into the shared scan pipeline.

stops.title: Inspect the local-model detector path
stops.description: src/detectors/nudenet.py:main() is the local inference workflow. It prompts for scan configuration, initializes NudeDetector, classifies images directly, classifies videos by frame extraction, reduces raw detections into simplified class/score data, and then hands normalized results to the shared result-handling utilities.
stops.nextStopReason: The companion detector shows how the same control flow is reused when inference happens over HTTP instead of in-process.

stops.title: Compare the API-backed detector path
stops.description: src/detectors/helloz_nsfw.py:main() mirrors the NudeNet flow but sends images and extracted video frames to an external Helloz NSFW service. This is the best place to understand the repository's abstraction boundary: scan orchestration and result handling stay shared, while only the model-specific classification step changes; docker-compose.yml provides the service this path expects.
stops.nextStopReason: Both detector paths converge in the shared result accumulator, which is where raw detections become durable report records.

stops.title: Follow the convergence point for scan outputs
stops.description: handle_results() turns detector output into report-ready data by generating thumbnails for flagged files, building ReportEntry-compatible dictionaries, appending them to the thread-safe in-memory report, and periodically triggering saves. This is the core integration point between detection, presentation, and persistence, so bugs in scan output usually pass through here.
stops.nextStopReason: Once results are normalized and buffered, the final important subsystem is the reporting/session layer that writes and reloads them.

stops.title: Finish with persistence and revisitability
stops.description: ReportManager in src/reporting/report_manager.py owns report I/O: writing Excel files with embedded thumbnails, saving session JSON, loading saved runs back into model objects, and validating report directories. This is the persistence boundary used by the CLI flows and by GUI features such as SessionMixin and ScanHistoryMixin, which let users reopen prior runs and export or manage scan history.
stops.nextStopReason: After this stop, you can branch into SessionMixin and ScanHistoryMixin if you want to focus on the saved-run experience inside the GUI.

review.summary: The assembled graph is comprehensive and correctly captures the major architectural components: entrypoints, core data models, utilities, detectors (NudeNet and Helloz NSFW), GUI mixin hierarchy, processing pipeline, and reporting layer. The main weaknesses are a reversed docker_compose edge direction, several missing call edges (notably run_gui->app.main, utils.save_nudity_report->ReportManager, ScanningMixin->FrameExtractor), and the absence of a NudeDetector external library node. With these fixes the graph would accurately reflect the full data flow from entrypoint through detection to report generation.
